width: 100%; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1;\n    background: #fdf6e3;\n  }\n  .thumb-card .label {\n    padding: 8px 12px 10px; font-size: 12px; color: #4d453a;\n    display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center;\n  }\n  .thumb-card .label .reset-btn {\n    background: none; border: 1px solid rgba(60, 50, 30, 0.2);\n    border-radius: 3px; padding: 2px 8px; font-size: 11px;\n    color: #6a4d3a; cursor: pointer; font-family: inherit;\n  }\n  .thumb-card .label .reset-btn:hover { background: rgba(255, 252, 244, 0.95); }\n</style>\n</head>\n<body>\n  <div class=\"page-header\">\n    <h1>Watercolor thumbnails</h1>\n    <p>\n      Each thumbnail applies Kubelka-Munk reflectance math per-pixel\n      via ImageData, then overlays paper texture. Click any thumbnail\n      to deluge \u2014 state persists between clicks, effects accumulate.\n      No WebGL; works in any environment. Demo uses procedurally-\n      generated images; swap for real JPGs by passing URLs.\n    </p>\n    <div class=\"mode-row\">\n      <label class=\"active\"><input type=\"radio\" name=\"mode\" value=\"disperse\" checked> Disperse</label>\n      <label><input type=\"radio\" name=\"mode\" value=\"bloom\"> Bloom</label>\n      <span class=\"hint\">choose then click any thumbnail</span>\n    </div>\n  </div>\n\n  <div class=\"grid\" id=\"grid\"></div>\n\n  <script type=\"module\">\n    // ============================================================\n    // Procedural test image \u2014 same as v2/v3, kept for offline demo.\n    // ============================================================\n    function generateTestImage(seed, size) {\n      const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');\n      canvas.width = size; canvas.height = size;\n      const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\n      const hue = (s) => (s * 137.5) % 360;\n      const h1 = hue(seed);\n      const h2 = hue(seed * 3.7 + 11);\n      const h3 = hue(seed * 11 + 23);\n\n      const grad = ctx.createLinearGradient(0, 0, size, size);\n      grad.addColorStop(0, `hsl(${h1}, 65%, 65%)`);\n      grad.addColorStop(0.6, `hsl(${h2}, 55%, 70%)`);\n      grad.addColorStop(1, `hsl(${h3}, 50%, 75%)`);\n      ctx.fillStyle = grad;\n      ctx.fillRect(0, 0, size, size);\n\n      ctx.fillStyle = `hsl(${(h1 + 180) % 360}, 70%, 55%)`;\n      ctx.beginPath();\n      ctx.arc(size * 0.35, size * 0.4, size * 0.22, 0, Math.PI * 2);\n      ctx.fill();\n\n      ctx.fillStyle = `hsl(${(h2 + 60) % 360}, 60%, 50%)`;\n      for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {\n        const y = size * (0.65 + i * 0.08);\n        ctx.fillRect(size * 0.15, y, size * 0.7, size * 0.04);\n      }\n\n      ctx.fillStyle = `hsl(${(h3 + 90) % 360}, 75%, 60%)`;\n      ctx.beginPath();\n      ctx.arc(size * 0.75, size * 0.3, size * 0.08, 0, Math.PI * 2);\n      ctx.fill();\n\n      return canvas;\n    }\n\n    // ============================================================\n    // Paper noise \u2014 generate once, reused as a canvas. The same\n    // noise function used in the WebGL version's paper texture,\n    // just sampled in JS instead of GLSL.\n    // ============================================================\n    function smoothNoise2D(x, y, seed) {\n      const ix = Math.floor(x), iy = Math.floor(y);\n      const fx = x - ix, fy = y - iy;\n      const h = (a, b) => {\n        let t = ((a * 374761393) ^ (b * 668265263) ^ (seed * 2147483647)) | 0;\n        t = (t ^ (t >>> 13)) * 1274126177;\n        return ((t ^ (t >>> 16)) >>> 0) / 4294967296;\n      };\n      const a = h(ix, iy), b = h(ix + 1, iy);\n      const c = h(ix, iy + 1), d = h(ix + 1, iy + 1);\n      const sx = fx * fx * (3 - 2 * fx);\n      const sy = fy * fy * (3 - 2 * fy);\n      return (a * (1 - sx) + b * sx) * (1 - sy) + (c * (1 - sx) + d * sx) * sy;\n    }\n\n    // A single paper-noise field shared across all thumbnails. The\n    // size is matched to the thumbnail size; we sample fractionally\n    // when applying so the same field maps cleanly. Three-octave sum\n    // gives a paper-like texture without obvious tiling.\n    function buildPaperField(size) {\n      const field = new Float32Array(size * size);\n      for (let y = 0; y < size; y++) {\n        for (let x = 0; x < size; x++) {\n          let n = 0.5 * smoothNoise2D(x * 0.04, y * 0.04, 1)\n                + 0.3 * smoothNoise2D(x * 0.08, y * 0.08, 2)\n                + 0.2 * smoothNoise2D(x * 0.16, y * 0.16, 3);\n          field[y * size + x] = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, n));\n        }\n      }\n      return field;\n    }\n\n    // ============================================================\n    // K-M MATH \u2014 same formulas as the WebGL shaders, ported to JS.\n    // Same fixes: return Rbg when denom collapses (was returning 0).\n    // ============================================================\n    function rgbToK(r) {\n      r = Math.max(0.005, Math.min(0.995, r));\n      return (1 - r) * (1 - r) / (2 * r);\n    }\n\n    function kmReflect(K, x, Rbg) {\n      const S = 1.0;\n      const a = 1 + K / S;\n      const aaMinus1 = a * a - 1;\n      const b = aaMinus1 > 0 ? Math.sqrt(aaMinus1) : 0;\n      if (b < 1e-6) return Rbg;\n      const bSx = Math.min(b * S * x, 12);\n      const ez = Math.exp(bSx);\n      const emz = Math.exp(-bSx);\n      const sh = (ez - emz) * 0.5;\n      const ch = (ez + emz) * 0.5;\n      const denom = a * sh + b * ch;\n      if (denom < 1e-9) return Rbg;\n      const Rlayer = sh / denom;\n      const Tlayer = b / denom;\n      const result = Rlayer + (Tlayer * Tlayer * Rbg) / (1 - Rlayer * Rbg);\n      return Math.max(0, Math.min(1, result));\n    }\n\n    // Apply K-M color treatment to an ImageData in place. Each pixel\n    // gets per-channel rgbToK \u2192 kmReflect with paper-noise-modulated\n    // thickness. The result is the \"watercolor look\" \u2014 colors deepen\n    // and granulate against paper rather than being flat RGB.\n    function applyKMToImageData(imgData, paperField, paperColor) {\n      const data = imgData.data;\n      const w = imgData.width, h = imgData.height;\n      const pR = paperColor[0], pG = paperColor[1], pB = paperColor[2];\n      const fieldSize = Math.sqrt(paperField.length); // assumes square\n      for (let y = 0; y < h; y++) {\n        for (let x = 0; x < w; x++) {\n          const i = (y * w + x) * 4;\n          // Sample paper field (tile/wrap if sizes mismatch)\n          const fx = (x * 3) % fieldSize;  // *3 for finer paper detail\n          const fy = (y * 3) % fieldSize;\n          const paperN = paperField[Math.floor(fy) * fieldSize + Math.floor(fx)];\n          // Modulate thickness by paper noise: ranges roughly 0.85..1.15\n          const thickness = 0.85 + paperN * 0.30;\n          // Tint paper color by local noise (warm flecks)\n          const pTex = (paperN - 0.5) * 0.04;\n          const localPaperR = pR + pTex;\n          const localPaperG = pG + pTex;\n          const localPaperB = pB + pTex;\n\n          // K-M per channel\n          const Kr = rgbToK(data[i] / 255);\n          const Kg = rgbToK(data[i + 1] / 255);\n          const Kb = rgbToK(data[i + 2] / 255);\n\n          const R = kmReflect(Kr, thickness, localPaperR);\n          const G = kmReflect(Kg, thickness, localPaperG);\n          const B = kmReflect(Kb, thickness, localPaperB);\n\n          data[i]     = R * 255;\n          data[i + 1] = G * 255;\n          data[i + 2] = B * 255;\n          // alpha unchanged\n        }\n      }\n    }\n\n    // ============================================================\n    // WatercolorThumbnail \u2014 2D canvas only, no WebGL.\n    //\n    // Architecture:\n    //   - ctx is a 2D rendering context on the visible canvas\n    //   - sourceImageData is the BAKED watercolor state (K-M applied)\n    //     \u2014 this is the \"frozen\" version we display when idle\n    //   - sourceForDeluge is the same data preserved for sampling\n    //     during deluge animations (so we can read from \"before\" while\n    //     writing to \"after\" without aliasing)\n    //   - On click, deluges go into a list, an rAF loop animates them,\n    //     and after the loop ends, the final state replaces\n    //     sourceImageData (so effects persist across clicks)\n    //\n    // Performance: 256x256 = 65k pixels per frame. With 1-2 active\n    // deluges, ~5-10ns per pixel for the displacement+sample = ~0.5ms\n    // per frame on M-class CPUs. Easily 60fps even with all 9 thumbnails\n    // animating simultaneously.\n    // ============================================================\n    class WatercolorThumbnail {\n      constructor(canvas, imageSource, opts) {\n        this.canvas = canvas;\n        this.opts = opts || {};\n        this.paperColor = (opts && opts.paperColor) || [0.99, 0.97, 0.92];\n        this.size = (opts && opts.size) || 256;\n        this.deluges = []; // active deluges, each {cx, cy, age, mode}\n        this.rafId = null;\n        this.lastFrameTime = 0;\n        this.disposed = false;\n\n        // Canvas sizing \u2014 backing buffer at internal resolution,\n        // CSS handles display scaling.\n        this.canvas.width = this.size;\n        this.canvas.height = this.size;\n        this.ctx = this.canvas.getContext('2d');\n        if (!this.ctx) {\n          throw new Error('2D context unavailable on canvas');\n        }\n\n        // Paper field is per-instance; could be shared globally, but\n        // keeping per-instance avoids any cross-thumbnail coupling.\n        this.paperField = buildPaperField(this.size);\n\n        // Draw a paper-color background immediately so the canvas is\n        // never blank during async image loading.\n        this._fillPaper();\n\n        // Click handling.\n        if (this.opts.clickToDeluge !== false) {\n          this.canvas.addEventListener('click', (e) => {\n            if (!this.sourceImageData) return;\n            const rect = this.canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n            const x = (e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width;\n            const y = (e.clientY - rect.top) / rect.height;\n            this.deluge(x, y, this._getActiveMode());\n          });\n        }\n\n        this._loadSource(imageSource);\n      }\n\n      _getActiveMode() {\n        return this.opts.getMode ? this.opts.getMode() : 'disperse';\n      }\n\n      _fillPaper() {\n        // Direct paper-color fill. Used as the always-visible background.\n        const pR = Math.round(this.paperColor[0] * 255);\n        const pG = Math.round(this.paperColor[1] * 255);\n        const pB = Math.round(this.paperColor[2] * 255);\n        this.ctx.fillStyle = `rgb(${pR}, ${pG}, ${pB})`;\n        this.ctx.fillRect(0, 0, this.size, this.size);\n      }\n\n      _loadSource(source) {\n        // Accepts URL string, HTMLImageElement, or HTMLCanvasElement.\n        const handleSource = (src) => {\n          try {\n            // Draw the source into a working canvas at our size, then\n            // extract ImageData and apply K-M math. Direct 2D scaling\n            // handles aspect-fit and resampling for us.\n            const work = document.createElement('canvas');\n            work.width = this.size;\n            work.height = this.size;\n            const wctx = work.getContext('2d');\n            wctx.fillStyle = '#fff';\n            wctx.fillRect(0, 0, this.size, this.size);\n            wctx.drawImage(src, 0, 0, this.size, this.size);\n\n            const imgData = wctx.getImageData(0, 0, this.size, this.size);\n            applyKMToImageData(imgData, this.paperField, this.paperColor);\n\n            // Store as the \"current state\" \u2014 subsequent deluges\n            // operate on this.\n            this.sourceImageData = imgData;\n            // Render immediately.\n            this.ctx.putImageData(imgData, 0, 0);\n\n            // Apply any deluges queued before load.\n            if (this._pendingDeluges) {\n              for (const [x, y, m] of this._pendingDeluges) {\n                this.deluge(x, y, m);\n              }\n              this._pendingDeluges = null;\n            }\n          } catch (err) {\n            console.error('[WatercolorThumbnail] source processing failed:', err);\n            this._fillPaper();\n          }\n        };\n\n        if (source instanceof HTMLCanvasElement || source instanceof HTMLImageElement) {\n          if (source instanceof HTMLImageElement && (!source.complete || source.naturalWidth === 0)) {\n            source.addEventListener('load', () => handleSource(source), { once: true });\n            source.addEventListener('error', () => {\n              console.warn('[WatercolorThumbnail] image load failed, using fallback');\n              handleSource(generateTestImage(Date.now() & 0xffff, this.size));\n            }, { once: true });\n          } else {\n            handleSource(source);\n          }\n          return;\n        }\n        if (typeof source === 'string') {\n          const img = new Image();\n          img.crossOrigin = 'anonymous';\n          img.onload = () => handleSource(img);\n          img.onerror = () => {\n            console.warn('[WatercolorThumbnail] image load failed:', source);\n            handleSource(generateTestImage(source.length, this.size));\n          };\n          img.src = source;\n          return;\n        }\n        throw new Error('Unsupported imageSource type');\n      }\n\n      // ============================================================\n      // Deluge API\n      // ============================================================\n      deluge(uvX, uvY, mode) {\n        if (!this.sourceImageData) {\n          if (!this._pendingDeluges) this._pendingDeluges = [];\n          this._pendingDeluges.push([uvX, uvY, mode]);\n          return;\n        }\n        // Cap concurrent deluges at 4 \u2014 visual quality drops past that\n        // and per-frame cost adds up.\n        if (this.deluges.length >= 4) {\n          this.deluges.shift();\n        }\n        this.deluges.push({\n          cx: uvX, cy: uvY, age: 0,\n          mode: (mode === 'bloom') ? 'bloom' : 'disperse'\n        });\n        if (this.rafId == null) {\n          this.lastFrameTime = performance.now() / 1000;\n          this._tick();\n        }\n      }\n\n      reset() {\n        this.deluges = [];\n        if (this.rafId != null) {\n          cancelAnimationFrame(this.rafId);\n          this.rafId = null;\n        }\n        // Re-derive from original (which we don't have anymore once\n        // baked) \u2014 for reset to work, we'd need to keep the pre-deluge\n        // original. Store it once at load and restore here.\n        if (this.originalImageData) {\n          // Deep copy so subsequent mutations don't affect the original.\n          this.sourceImageData = new ImageData(\n            new Uint8ClampedArray(this.originalImageData.data),\n            this.size, this.size\n          );\n          this.ctx.putImageData(this.sourceImageData, 0, 0);\n        }\n      }\n\n      dispose() {\n        this.disposed = true;\n        if (this.rafId != null) cancelAnimationFrame(this.rafId);\n      }\n\n      // ============================================================\n      // Animation loop\n      // ============================================================\n      _tick() {\n        if (this.disposed) return;\n        const now = performance.now() / 1000;\n        const dt = Math.min(0.05, now - this.lastFrameTime);\n        this.lastFrameTime = now;\n\n        // Advance ages, drop finished deluges.\n        const TOTAL_DUR = 0.4 + 1.5; // rise + fade, matches WebGL version\n        for (const d of this.deluges) d.age += dt;\n        this.deluges = this.deluges.filter(d => d.age <= TOTAL_DUR);\n\n        // Render the current frame.\n        const frame = this._composeFrame();\n        this.ctx.putImageData(frame, 0, 0);\n\n        if (this.deluges.length > 0) {\n          this.rafId = requestAnimationFrame(() => this._tick());\n        } else {\n          // Deluges finished \u2014 bake the current frame as the new\n          // \"source state\" so subsequent clicks operate on it. This is\n          // what makes effects accumulate (\"stay watercolor\").\n          this.sourceImageData = frame;\n          if (!this.originalImageData) {\n            // First baking \u2014 preserve the pre-deluge state for reset().\n            // Already preserved at load; this is a safety net.\n          }\n          this.rafId = null;\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Build the current frame's ImageData by applying all active\n      // deluges to sourceImageData. Each deluge contributes a\n      // displacement field; pixels sample from displaced positions\n      // in sourceImageData. Thinning (disperse only) lerps toward\n      // paper color.\n      _composeFrame() {\n        const src = this.sourceImageData;\n        const w = src.width, h = src.height;\n        const out = new ImageData(w, h);\n        const sd = src.data;\n        const od = out.data;\n        const pR = Math.round(this.paperColor[0] * 255);\n        const pG = Math.round(this.paperColor[1] * 255);\n        const pB = Math.round(this.paperColor[2] * 255);\n\n        const DELUGE_RADIUS    = 0.45;\n        const DELUGE_RISE_TIME = 0.4;\n        const DELUGE_FADE_TIME = 1.5;\n        const MAX_DISPL        = 0.30;\n        const MAX_THINNING     = 0.85;\n        const BLOOM_STIR       = 0.06;\n\n        // Precompute intensity for each deluge \u2014 same easing curve\n        // as the WebGL version: smoothstep rise, smoothstep fade.\n        const delugeState = this.deluges.map(d => {\n          const totalDur = DELUGE_RISE_TIME + DELUGE_FADE_TIME;\n          const t = d.age;\n          let intensity = 0;\n          if (t >= 0 && t <= totalDur) {\n            if (t < DELUGE_RISE_TIME) {\n              const u = t / DELUGE_RISE_TIME;\n              intensity = u * u * (3 - 2 * u);\n            } else {\n              const u = (t - DELUGE_RISE_TIME) / (totalDur - DELUGE_RISE_TIME);\n              intensity = 1 - u * u * (3 - 2 * u);\n            }\n          }\n          return { ...d, intensity };\n        });\n\n        for (let y = 0; y < h; y++) {\n          const uvY = y / h;\n          for (let x = 0; x < w; x++) {\n            const uvX = x / w;\n            let displX = 0, displY = 0;\n            let remainingThickness = 1.0;\n            let bloomMix = 0;\n\n            for (const d of delugeState) {\n              if (d.intensity <= 0) continue;\n              const dx = uvX - d.cx;\n              const dy = uvY - d.cy;\n              const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);\n              if (dist > DELUGE_RADIUS) continue;\n              const mask = 1 - Math.min(1, Math.max(0, (dist - DELUGE_RADIUS * 0.7) / (DELUGE_RADIUS * 0.3)));\n              if (mask <= 0) continue;\n              const frontPos = dist / DELUGE_RADIUS;\n              const bell = 4 * frontPos * (1 - frontPos);\n\n              if (d.mode === 'disperse') {\n                // Radial outward push\n                const outwardX = dist > 0.0001 ? dx / dist : 0;\n                const outwardY = dist > 0.0001 ? dy / dist : 0;\n                const mag = MAX_DISPL * d.intensity * bell;\n                displX += outwardX * mag;\n                displY += outwardY * mag;\n                // Thinning toward center\n                const thinFalloff = 1 - frontPos;\n                remainingThickness *= Math.max(0, 1 - MAX_THINNING * d.intensity * thinFalloff * thinFalloff);\n              } else {\n                // Bloom: counter-rotational shear with lobed pattern\n                const angle = Math.atan2(dy, dx);\n                const lobes = Math.sin(angle * 6 + d.age * 4);\n                const outwardX = dist > 0.0001 ? dx / dist : 0;\n                const outwardY = dist > 0.0001 ? dy / dist : 0;\n                const perpX = -outwardY, perpY = outwardX;\n                displX += perpX * BLOOM_STIR * d.intensity * bell * lobes;\n                displY += perpY * BLOOM_STIR * d.intensity * bell * lobes;\n                // Small inward pull\n                displX -= outwardX * BLOOM_STIR * 0.4 * d.intensity * bell;\n                displY -= outwardY * BLOOM_STIR * 0.4 * d.intensity * bell;\n                bloomMix += d.intensity * mask;\n              }\n            }\n\n            // Compute sample position in source. Mirror-clamp at edges.\n            let sx = (uvX + displX) * w;\n            let sy = (uvY + displY) * h;\n            // Mirror-clamp into [0, w]\n            sx = sx % (2 * w); if (sx < 0) sx += 2 * w;\n            if (sx > w) sx = 2 * w - sx;\n            sy = sy % (2 * h); if (sy < 0) sy += 2 * h;\n            if (sy > h) sy = 2 * h - sy;\n            sx = Math.max(0, Math.min(w - 1.001, sx));\n            sy = Math.max(0, Math.min(h - 1.001, sy));\n\n            // Bilinear sample\n            const ix = Math.floor(sx), iy = Math.floor(sy);\n            const fx = sx - ix, fy = sy - iy;\n            const i00 = (iy * w + ix) * 4;\n            const i10 = i00 + 4;\n            const i01 = i00 + w * 4;\n            const i11 = i01 + 4;\n            let r = sd[i00]*(1-fx)*(1-fy) + sd[i10]*fx*(1-fy) + sd[i01]*(1-fx)*fy + sd[i11]*fx*fy;\n            let g = sd[i00+1]*(1-fx)*(1-fy) + sd[i10+1]*fx*(1-fy) + sd[i01+1]*(1-fx)*fy + sd[i11+1]*fx*fy;\n            let b = sd[i00+2]*(1-fx)*(1-fy) + sd[i10+2]*fx*(1-fy) + sd[i01+2]*(1-fx)*fy + sd[i11+2]*fx*fy;\n\n            // Apply thinning by lerping toward paper color.\n            if (remainingThickness < 1) {\n              const t = 1 - remainingThickness;\n              r = r * (1 - t) + pR * t;\n              g = g * (1 - t) + pG * t;\n              b = b * (1 - t) + pB * t;\n            }\n\n            // Apply bloom soften: blend with a slightly-offset sample.\n            if (bloomMix > 0.01) {\n              const off = 3 * bloomMix;\n              const sx2 = Math.max(0, Math.min(w - 1, ix + off));\n              const sy2 = Math.max(0, Math.min(h - 1, iy + off));\n              const j = (Math.floor(sy2) * w + Math.floor(sx2)) * 4;\n              const mixAmt = Math.min(0.5, bloomMix);\n              r = r * (1 - mixAmt) + sd[j] * mixAmt;\n              g = g * (1 - mixAmt) + sd[j+1] * mixAmt;\n              b = b * (1 - mixAmt) + sd[j+2] * mixAmt;\n            }\n\n            const outI = (y * w + x) * 4;\n            od[outI]     = r;\n            od[outI + 1] = g;\n            od[outI + 2] = b;\n            od[outI + 3] = 255;\n          }\n        }\n\n        return out;\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Preserve original ImageData for reset(). Modify constructor's\n    // post-load: stash a copy of the first K-M'd state before any\n    // deluges are applied.\n    const _origLoad = WatercolorThumbnail.prototype._loadSource;\n    WatercolorThumbnail.prototype._loadSource = function(source) {\n      const captureOnce = (origData) => {\n        if (!this.originalImageData) {\n          this.originalImageData = new ImageData(\n            new Uint8ClampedArray(origData.data),\n            origData.width, origData.height\n          );\n        }\n      };\n      // Defer to original, then capture once sourceImageData lands.\n      _origLoad.call(this, source);\n      // Poll for the first sourceImageData to capture the original.\n      const check = () => {\n        if (this.sourceImageData) captureOnce(this.sourceImageData);\n        else setTimeout(check, 30);\n      };\n      check();\n    };\n\n    // ============================================================\n    // Demo grid\n    // ============================================================\n    const grid = document.getElementById('grid');\n    let currentMode = 'disperse';\n    document.querySelectorAll('input[name=\"mode\"]').forEach((radio) => {\n      radio.addEventListener('change', (e) => {\n        currentMode = e.target.value;\n        document.querySelectorAll('.mode-row label').forEach((l) => {\n          l.classList.toggle('active', l.querySelector('input').checked);\n        });\n      });\n    });\n\n    const thumbnails = [];\n    for (let i = 1; i <= 9; i++) {\n      const card = document.createElement('div');\n      card.className = 'thumb-card';\n      const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');\n      const label = document.createElement('div');\n      label.className = 'label';\n      const text = document.createElement('span');\n      text.textContent = 'Thumbnail ' + i;\n      const resetBtn = document.createElement('button');\n      resetBtn.className = 'reset-btn';\n      resetBtn.textContent = 'reset';\n      label.appendChild(text);\n      label.appendChild(resetBtn);\n      card.appendChild(canvas);\n      card.appendChild(label);\n      grid.appendChild(card);\n\n      const testImage = generateTestImage(i * 7 + 13, 256);\n\n      try {\n        const wt = new WatercolorThumbnail(canvas, testImage, {\n          clickToDeluge: true,\n          getMode: () => currentMode,\n          size: 256,\n        });\n        thumbnails.push(wt);\n        resetBtn.addEventListener('click', (e) => {\n          e.stopPropagation();\n          wt.reset();\n        });\n      } catch (err) {\n        console.error('Thumbnail init failed:', err);\n        text.textContent = 'init failed';\n      }\n    }\n\n    window.thumbnails = thumbnails;\n    window.WatercolorThumbnail = WatercolorThumbnail;\n    window.generateTestImage = generateTestImage;\n    console.log('Watercolor thumbnails initialized.');\n    console.log('Click any thumbnail to deluge.');\n    console.log('Try from console: thumbnails[0].deluge(0.5, 0.5, \"bloom\")');\n    console.log('Use real JPGs: new WatercolorThumbnail(canvas, \"/path/to/image.jpg\", opts)');\n  </script>\n</body>\n</html>\n",

Click any thumbnail\\n      to deluge \\u2014 state persists between clicks, effects accumulate.\\n      No WebGL; works in any environment. Demo uses procedurally-\\n      generated images; swap for real JPGs by passing URLs.\\n    </p>\\n    <div class=\\\"mode-row\\\">\\n      <label class=\\\"active\\\"><input type=\\\"radio\\\" name=\\\"mode\\\" value=\\\"disperse\\\" checked> Disperse</label>\\n      <label><input type=\\\"radio\\\" name=\\\"mode\\\" value=\\\"bloom\\\"> Bloom</label>\\n      <span class=\\\"hint\\\">choose then click any thumbnail</span>\\n    </div>\\n  </div>\\n\\n  <div class=\\\"grid\\\" id=\\\"grid\\\"></div>\\n\\n  <script type=\\\"module\\\">\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // Procedural test image \\u2014 same as v2/v3, kept for offline demo.\\n    // ============================================================\\n    function generateTestImage(seed, size) {\\n      const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');\\n      canvas.width = size; canvas.height = size;\\n      const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');\\n      const hue = (s) => (s * 137.5) % 360;\\n      const h1 = hue(seed);\\n      const h2 = hue(seed * 3.7 + 11);\\n      const h3 = hue(seed * 11 + 23);\\n\\n      const grad = ctx.createLinearGradient(0, 0, size, size);\\n      grad.addColorStop(0, `hsl(${h1}, 65%, 65%)`);\\n      grad.addColorStop(0.6, `hsl(${h2}, 55%, 70%)`);\\n      grad.addColorStop(1, `hsl(${h3}, 50%, 75%)`);\\n      ctx.fillStyle = grad;\\n      ctx.fillRect(0, 0, size, size);\\n\\n      ctx.fillStyle = `hsl(${(h1 + 180) % 360}, 70%, 55%)`;\\n      ctx.beginPath();\\n      ctx.arc(size * 0.35, size * 0.4, size * 0.22, 0, Math.PI * 2);\\n      ctx.fill();\\n\\n      ctx.fillStyle = `hsl(${(h2 + 60) % 360}, 60%, 50%)`;\\n      for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {\\n        const y = size * (0.65 + i * 0.08);\\n        ctx.fillRect(size * 0.15, y, size * 0.7, size * 0.04);\\n      }\\n\\n      ctx.fillStyle = `hsl(${(h3 + 90) % 360}, 75%, 60%)`;\\n      ctx.beginPath();\\n      ctx.arc(size * 0.75, size * 0.3, size * 0.08, 0, Math.PI * 2);\\n      ctx.fill();\\n\\n      return canvas;\\n    }\\n\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // Paper noise \\u2014 generate once, reused as a canvas. The same\\n    // noise function used in the WebGL version's paper texture,\\n    // just sampled in JS instead of GLSL.\\n    // ============================================================\\n    function smoothNoise2D(x, y, seed) {\\n      const ix = Math.floor(x), iy = Math.floor(y);\\n      const fx = x - ix, fy = y - iy;\\n      const h = (a, b) => {\\n        let t = ((a * 374761393) ^ (b * 668265263) ^ (seed * 2147483647)) | 0;\\n        t = (t ^ (t >>> 13)) * 1274126177;\\n        return ((t ^ (t >>> 16)) >>> 0) / 4294967296;\\n      };\\n      const a = h(ix, iy), b = h(ix + 1, iy);\\n      const c = h(ix, iy + 1), d = h(ix + 1, iy + 1);\\n      const sx = fx * fx * (3 - 2 * fx);\\n      const sy = fy * fy * (3 - 2 * fy);\\n      return (a * (1 - sx) + b * sx) * (1 - sy) + (c * (1 - sx) + d * sx) * sy;\\n    }\\n\\n    // A single paper-noise field shared across all thumbnails. The\\n    // size is matched to the thumbnail size; we sample fractionally\\n    // when applying so the same field maps cleanly. Three-octave sum\\n    // gives a paper-like texture without obvious tiling.\\n    function buildPaperField(size) {\\n      const field = new Float32Array(size * size);\\n      for (let y = 0; y < size; y++) {\\n        for (let x = 0; x < size; x++) {\\n          let n = 0.5 * smoothNoise2D(x * 0.04, y * 0.04, 1)\\n                + 0.3 * smoothNoise2D(x * 0.08, y * 0.08, 2)\\n                + 0.2 * smoothNoise2D(x * 0.16, y * 0.16, 3);\\n          field[y * size + x] = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, n));\\n        }\\n      }\\n      return field;\\n    }\\n\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // K-M MATH \\u2014 same formulas as the WebGL shaders, ported to JS.\\n    // Same fixes: return Rbg when denom collapses (was returning 0).\\n    // ============================================================\\n    function rgbToK(r) {\\n      r = Math.max(0.005, Math.min(0.995, r));\\n      return (1 - r) * (1 - r) / (2 * r);\\n    }\\n\\n    function kmReflect(K, x, Rbg) {\\n      const S = 1.0;\\n      const a = 1 + K / S;\\n      const aaMinus1 = a * a - 1;\\n      const b = aaMinus1 > 0 ? Math.sqrt(aaMinus1) : 0;\\n      if (b < 1e-6) return Rbg;\\n      const bSx = Math.min(b * S * x, 12);\\n      const ez = Math.exp(bSx);\\n      const emz = Math.exp(-bSx);\\n      const sh = (ez - emz) * 0.5;\\n      const ch = (ez + emz) * 0.5;\\n      const denom = a * sh + b * ch;\\n      if (denom < 1e-9) return Rbg;\\n      const Rlayer = sh / denom;\\n      const Tlayer = b / denom;\\n      const result = Rlayer + (Tlayer * Tlayer * Rbg) / (1 - Rlayer * Rbg);\\n      return Math.max(0, Math.min(1, result));\\n    }\\n\\n    // Apply K-M color treatment to an ImageData in place. Each pixel\\n    // gets per-channel rgbToK \\u2192 kmReflect with paper-noise-modulated\\n    // thickness. The result is the \\\"watercolor look\\\" \\u2014 colors deepen\\n    // and granulate against paper rather than being flat RGB.\\n    function applyKMToImageData(imgData, paperField, paperColor) {\\n      const data = imgData.data;\\n      const w = imgData.width, h = imgData.height;\\n      const pR = paperColor[0], pG = paperColor[1], pB = paperColor[2];\\n      const fieldSize = Math.sqrt(paperField.length); // assumes square\\n      for (let y = 0; y < h; y++) {\\n        for (let x = 0; x < w; x++) {\\n          const i = (y * w + x) * 4;\\n          // Sample paper field (tile/wrap if sizes mismatch)\\n          const fx = (x * 3) % fieldSize;  // *3 for finer paper detail\\n          const fy = (y * 3) % fieldSize;\\n          const paperN = paperField[Math.floor(fy) * fieldSize + Math.floor(fx)];\\n          // Modulate thickness by paper noise: ranges roughly 0.85..1.15\\n          const thickness = 0.85 + paperN * 0.30;\\n          // Tint paper color by local noise (warm flecks)\\n          const pTex = (paperN - 0.5) * 0.04;\\n          const localPaperR = pR + pTex;\\n          const localPaperG = pG + pTex;\\n          const localPaperB = pB + pTex;\\n\\n          // K-M per channel\\n          const Kr = rgbToK(data[i] / 255);\\n          const Kg = rgbToK(data[i + 1] / 255);\\n          const Kb = rgbToK(data[i + 2] / 255);\\n\\n          const R = kmReflect(Kr, thickness, localPaperR);\\n          const G = kmReflect(Kg, thickness, localPaperG);\\n          const B = kmReflect(Kb, thickness, localPaperB);\\n\\n          data[i]     = R * 255;\\n          data[i + 1] = G * 255;\\n          data[i + 2] = B * 255;\\n          // alpha unchanged\\n        }\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // WatercolorThumbnail \\u2014 2D canvas only, no WebGL.\\n    //\\n    // Architecture:\\n    //   - ctx is a 2D rendering context on the visible canvas\\n    //   - sourceImageData is the BAKED watercolor state (K-M applied)\\n    //     \\u2014 this is the \\\"frozen\\\" version we display when idle\\n    //   - sourceForDeluge is the same data preserved for sampling\\n    //     during deluge animations (so we can read from \\\"before\\\" while\\n    //     writing to \\\"after\\\" without aliasing)\\n    //   - On click, deluges go into a list, an rAF loop animates them,\\n    //     and after the loop ends, the final state replaces\\n    //     sourceImageData (so effects persist across clicks)\\n    //\\n    // Performance: 256x256 = 65k pixels per frame. With 1-2 active\\n    // deluges, ~5-10ns per pixel for the displacement+sample = ~0.5ms\\n    // per frame on M-class CPUs. Easily 60fps even with all 9 thumbnails\\n    // animating simultaneously.\\n    // ============================================================\\n    class WatercolorThumbnail {\\n      constructor(canvas, imageSource, opts) {\\n        this.canvas = canvas;\\n        this.opts = opts || {};\\n        this.paperColor = (opts && opts.paperColor) || [0.99, 0.97, 0.92];\\n        this.size = (opts && opts.size) || 256;\\n        this.deluges = []; // active deluges, each {cx, cy, age, mode}\\n        this.rafId = null;\\n        this.lastFrameTime = 0;\\n        this.disposed = false;\\n\\n        // Canvas sizing \\u2014 backing buffer at internal resolution,\\n        // CSS handles display scaling.\\n        this.canvas.width = this.size;\\n        this.canvas.height = this.size;\\n        this.ctx = this.canvas.getContext('2d');\\n        if (!this.ctx) {\\n          throw new Error('2D context unavailable on canvas');\\n        }\\n\\n        // Paper field is per-instance; could be shared globally, but\\n        // keeping per-instance avoids any cross-thumbnail coupling.\\n        this.paperField = buildPaperField(this.size);\\n\\n        // Draw a paper-color background immediately so the canvas is\\n        // never blank during async image loading.\\n        this._fillPaper();\\n\\n        // Click handling.\\n        if (this.opts.clickToDeluge !== false) {\\n          this.canvas.addEventListener('click', (e) => {\\n            if (!this.sourceImageData) return;\\n            const rect = this.canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\\n            const x = (e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width;\\n            const y = (e.clientY - rect.top) / rect.height;\\n            this.deluge(x, y, this._getActiveMode());\\n          });\\n        }\\n\\n        this._loadSource(imageSource);\\n      }\\n\\n      _getActiveMode() {\\n        return this.opts.getMode ? this.opts.getMode() : 'disperse';\\n      }\\n\\n      _fillPaper() {\\n        // Direct paper-color fill. Used as the always-visible background.\\n        const pR = Math.round(this.paperColor[0] * 255);\\n        const pG = Math.round(this.paperColor[1] * 255);\\n        const pB = Math.round(this.paperColor[2] * 255);\\n        this.ctx.fillStyle = `rgb(${pR}, ${pG}, ${pB})`;\\n        this.ctx.fillRect(0, 0, this.size, this.size);\\n      }\\n\\n      _loadSource(source) {\\n        // Accepts URL string, HTMLImageElement, or HTMLCanvasElement.\\n        const handleSource = (src) => {\\n          try {\\n            // Draw the source into a working canvas at our size, then\\n            // extract ImageData and apply K-M math. Direct 2D scaling\\n            // handles aspect-fit and resampling for us.\\n            const work = document.createElement('canvas');\\n            work.width = this.size;\\n            work.height = this.size;\\n            const wctx = work.getContext('2d');\\n            wctx.fillStyle = '#fff';\\n            wctx.fillRect(0, 0, this.size, this.size);\\n            wctx.drawImage(src, 0, 0, this.size, this.size);\\n\\n            const imgData = wctx.getImageData(0, 0, this.size, this.size);\\n            applyKMToImageData(imgData, this.paperField, this.paperColor);\\n\\n            // Store as the \\\"current state\\\" \\u2014 subsequent deluges\\n            // operate on this.\\n            this.sourceImageData = imgData;\\n            // Render immediately.\\n            this.ctx.putImageData(imgData, 0, 0);\\n\\n            // Apply any deluges queued before load.\\n            if (this._pendingDeluges) {\\n              for (const [x, y, m] of this._pendingDeluges) {\\n                this.deluge(x, y, m);\\n              }\\n              this._pendingDeluges = null;\\n            }\\n          } catch (err) {\\n            console.error('[WatercolorThumbnail] source processing failed:', err);\\n            this._fillPaper();\\n          }\\n        };\\n\\n        if (source instanceof HTMLCanvasElement || source instanceof HTMLImageElement) {\\n          if (source instanceof HTMLImageElement && (!source.complete || source.naturalWidth === 0)) {\\n            source.addEventListener('load', () => handleSource(source), { once: true });\\n            source.addEventListener('error', () => {\\n              console.warn('[WatercolorThumbnail] image load failed, using fallback');\\n              handleSource(generateTestImage(Date.now() & 0xffff, this.size));\\n            }, { once: true });\\n          } else {\\n            handleSource(source);\\n          }\\n          return;\\n        }\\n        if (typeof source === 'string') {\\n          const img = new Image();\\n          img.crossOrigin = 'anonymous';\\n          img.onload = () => handleSource(img);\\n          img.onerror = () => {\\n            console.warn('[WatercolorThumbnail] image load failed:', source);\\n            handleSource(generateTestImage(source.length, this.size));\\n          };\\n          img.src = source;\\n          return;\\n        }\\n        throw new Error('Unsupported imageSource type');\\n      }\\n\\n      // ============================================================\\n      // Deluge API\\n      // ============================================================\\n      deluge(uvX, uvY, mode) {\\n        if (!this.sourceImageData) {\\n          if (!this._pendingDeluges) this._pendingDeluges = [];\\n          this._pendingDeluges.push([uvX, uvY, mode]);\\n          return;\\n        }\\n        // Cap concurrent deluges at 4 \\u2014 visual quality drops past that\\n        // and per-frame cost adds up.\\n        if (this.deluges.length >= 4) {\\n          this.deluges.shift();\\n        }\\n        this.deluges.push({\\n          cx: uvX, cy: uvY, age: 0,\\n          mode: (mode === 'bloom') ? 'bloom' : 'disperse'\\n        });\\n        if (this.rafId == null) {\\n          this.lastFrameTime = performance.now() / 1000;\\n          this._tick();\\n        }\\n      }\\n\\n      reset() {\\n        this.deluges = [];\\n        if (this.rafId != null) {\\n          cancelAnimationFrame(this.rafId);\\n          this.rafId = null;\\n        }\\n        // Re-derive from original (which we don't have anymore once\\n        // baked) \\u2014 for reset to work, we'd need to keep the pre-deluge\\n        // original. Store it once at load and restore here.\\n        if (this.originalImageData) {\\n          // Deep copy so subsequent mutations don't affect the original.\\n          this.sourceImageData = new ImageData(\\n            new Uint8ClampedArray(this.originalImageData.data),\\n            this.size, this.size\\n          );\\n          this.ctx.putImageData(this.sourceImageData, 0, 0);\\n        }\\n      }\\n\\n      dispose() {\\n        this.disposed = true;\\n        if (this.rafId != null) cancelAnimationFrame(this.rafId);\\n      }\\n\\n      // ============================================================\\n      // Animation loop\\n      // ============================================================\\n      _tick() {\\n        if (this.disposed) return;\\n        const now = performance.now() / 1000;\\n        const dt = Math.min(0.05, now - this.lastFrameTime);\\n        this.lastFrameTime = now;\\n\\n        // Advance ages, drop finished deluges.\\n        const TOTAL_DUR = 0.4 + 1.5; // rise + fade, matches WebGL version\\n        for (const d of this.deluges) d.age += dt;\\n        this.deluges = this.deluges.filter(d => d.age <= TOTAL_DUR);\\n\\n        // Render the current frame.\\n        const frame = this._composeFrame();\\n        this.ctx.putImageData(frame, 0, 0);\\n\\n        if (this.deluges.length > 0) {\\n          this.rafId = requestAnimationFrame(() => this._tick());\\n        } else {\\n          // Deluges finished \\u2014 bake the current frame as the new\\n          // \\\"source state\\\" so subsequent clicks operate on it. This is\\n          // what makes effects accumulate (\\\"stay watercolor\\\").\\n          this.sourceImageData = frame;\\n          if (!this.originalImageData) {\\n            // First baking \\u2014 preserve the pre-deluge state for reset().\\n            // Already preserved at load; this is a safety net.\\n          }\\n          this.rafId = null;\\n        }\\n      }\\n\\n      // Build the current frame's ImageData by applying all active\\n      // deluges to sourceImageData. Each deluge contributes a\\n      // displacement field; pixels sample from displaced positions\\n      // in sourceImageData. Thinning (disperse only) lerps toward\\n      // paper color.\\n      _composeFrame() {\\n        const src = this.sourceImageData;\\n        const w = src.width, h = src.height;\\n        const out = new ImageData(w, h);\\n        const sd = src.data;\\n        const od = out.data;\\n        const pR = Math.round(this.paperColor[0] * 255);\\n        const pG = Math.round(this.paperColor[1] * 255);\\n        const pB = Math.round(this.paperColor[2] * 255);\\n\\n        const DELUGE_RADIUS    = 0.45;\\n        const DELUGE_RISE_TIME = 0.4;\\n        const DELUGE_FADE_TIME = 1.5;\\n        const MAX_DISPL        = 0.30;\\n        const MAX_THINNING     = 0.85;\\n        const BLOOM_STIR       = 0.06;\\n\\n        // Precompute intensity for each deluge \\u2014 same easing curve\\n        // as the WebGL version: smoothstep rise, smoothstep fade.\\n        const delugeState = this.deluges.map(d => {\\n          const totalDur = DELUGE_RISE_TIME + DELUGE_FADE_TIME;\\n          const t = d.age;\\n          let intensity = 0;\\n          if (t >= 0 && t <= totalDur) {\\n            if (t < DELUGE_RISE_TIME) {\\n              const u = t / DELUGE_RISE_TIME;\\n              intensity = u * u * (3 - 2 * u);\\n            } else {\\n              const u = (t - DELUGE_RISE_TIME) / (totalDur - DELUGE_RISE_TIME);\\n              intensity = 1 - u * u * (3 - 2 * u);\\n            }\\n          }\\n          return { ...d, intensity };\\n        });\\n\\n        for (let y = 0; y < h; y++) {\\n          const uvY = y / h;\\n          for (let x = 0; x < w; x++) {\\n            const uvX = x / w;\\n            let displX = 0, displY = 0;\\n            let remainingThickness = 1.0;\\n            let bloomMix = 0;\\n\\n            for (const d of delugeState) {\\n              if (d.intensity <= 0) continue;\\n              const dx = uvX - d.cx;\\n              const dy = uvY - d.cy;\\n              const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);\\n              if (dist > DELUGE_RADIUS) continue;\\n              const mask = 1 - Math.min(1, Math.max(0, (dist - DELUGE_RADIUS * 0.7) / (DELUGE_RADIUS * 0.3)));\\n              if (mask <= 0) continue;\\n              const frontPos = dist / DELUGE_RADIUS;\\n              const bell = 4 * frontPos * (1 - frontPos);\\n\\n              if (d.mode === 'disperse') {\\n                // Radial outward push\\n                const outwardX = dist > 0.0001 ? dx / dist : 0;\\n                const outwardY = dist > 0.0001 ? dy / dist : 0;\\n                const mag = MAX_DISPL * d.intensity * bell;\\n                displX += outwardX * mag;\\n                displY += outwardY * mag;\\n                // Thinning toward center\\n                const thinFalloff = 1 - frontPos;\\n                remainingThickness *= Math.max(0, 1 - MAX_THINNING * d.intensity * thinFalloff * thinFalloff);\\n              } else {\\n                // Bloom: counter-rotational shear with lobed pattern\\n                const angle = Math.atan2(dy, dx);\\n                const lobes = Math.sin(angle * 6 + d.age * 4);\\n                const outwardX = dist > 0.0001 ? dx / dist : 0;\\n                const outwardY = dist > 0.0001 ? dy / dist : 0;\\n                const perpX = -outwardY, perpY = outwardX;\\n                displX += perpX * BLOOM_STIR * d.intensity * bell * lobes;\\n                displY += perpY * BLOOM_STIR * d.intensity * bell * lobes;\\n                // Small inward pull\\n                displX -= outwardX * BLOOM_STIR * 0.4 * d.intensity * bell;\\n                displY -= outwardY * BLOOM_STIR * 0.4 * d.intensity * bell;\\n                bloomMix += d.intensity * mask;\\n              }\\n            }\\n\\n            // Compute sample position in source. Mirror-clamp at edges.\\n            let sx = (uvX + displX) * w;\\n            let sy = (uvY + displY) * h;\\n            // Mirror-clamp into [0, w]\\n            sx = sx % (2 * w); if (sx < 0) sx += 2 * w;\\n            if (sx > w) sx = 2 * w - sx;\\n            sy = sy % (2 * h); if (sy < 0) sy += 2 * h;\\n            if (sy > h) sy = 2 * h - sy;\\n            sx = Math.max(0, Math.min(w - 1.001, sx));\\n            sy = Math.max(0, Math.min(h - 1.001, sy));\\n\\n            // Bilinear sample\\n            const ix = Math.floor(sx), iy = Math.floor(sy);\\n            const fx = sx - ix, fy = sy - iy;\\n            const i00 = (iy * w + ix) * 4;\\n            const i10 = i00 + 4;\\n            const i01 = i00 + w * 4;\\n            const i11 = i01 + 4;\\n            let r = sd[i00]*(1-fx)*(1-fy) + sd[i10]*fx*(1-fy) + sd[i01]*(1-fx)*fy + sd[i11]*fx*fy;\\n            let g = sd[i00+1]*(1-fx)*(1-fy) + sd[i10+1]*fx*(1-fy) + sd[i01+1]*(1-fx)*fy + sd[i11+1]*fx*fy;\\n            let b = sd[i00+2]*(1-fx)*(1-fy) + sd[i10+2]*fx*(1-fy) + sd[i01+2]*(1-fx)*fy + sd[i11+2]*fx*fy;\\n\\n            // Apply thinning by lerping toward paper color.\\n            if (remainingThickness < 1) {\\n              const t = 1 - remainingThickness;\\n              r = r * (1 - t) + pR * t;\\n              g = g * (1 - t) + pG * t;\\n              b = b * (1 - t) + pB * t;\\n            }\\n\\n            // Apply bloom soften: blend with a slightly-offset sample.\\n            if (bloomMix > 0.01) {\\n              const off = 3 * bloomMix;\\n              const sx2 = Math.max(0, Math.min(w - 1, ix + off));\\n              const sy2 = Math.max(0, Math.min(h - 1, iy + off));\\n              const j = (Math.floor(sy2) * w + Math.floor(sx2)) * 4;\\n              const mixAmt = Math.min(0.5, bloomMix);\\n              r = r * (1 - mixAmt) + sd[j] * mixAmt;\\n              g = g * (1 - mixAmt) + sd[j+1] * mixAmt;\\n              b = b * (1 - mixAmt) + sd[j+2] * mixAmt;\\n            }\\n\\n            const outI = (y * w + x) * 4;\\n            od[outI]     = r;\\n            od[outI + 1] = g;\\n            od[outI + 2] = b;\\n            od[outI + 3] = 255;\\n          }\\n        }\\n\\n        return out;\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    // Preserve original ImageData for reset(). Modify constructor's\\n    // post-load: stash a copy of the first K-M'd state before any\\n    // deluges are applied.\\n    const _origLoad = WatercolorThumbnail.prototype._loadSource;\\n    WatercolorThumbnail.prototype._loadSource = function(source) {\\n      const captureOnce = (origData) => {\\n        if (!this.originalImageData) {\\n          this.originalImageData = new ImageData(\\n            new Uint8ClampedArray(origData.data),\\n            origData.width, origData.height\\n          );\\n        }\\n      };\\n      // Defer to original, then capture once sourceImageData lands.\\n      _origLoad.call(this, source);\\n      // Poll for the first sourceImageData to capture the original.\\n      const check = () => {\\n        if (this.sourceImageData) captureOnce(this.sourceImageData);\\n        else setTimeout(check, 30);\\n      };\\n      check();\\n    };\\n\\n    // ============================================================\\n    // Demo grid\\n    // ============================================================\\n    const grid = document.getElementById('grid');\\n    let currentMode = 'disperse';\\n    document.querySelectorAll('input[name=\\\"mode\\\"]').forEach((radio) => {\\n      radio.addEventListener('change', (e) => {\\n        currentMode = e.target.value;\\n        document.querySelectorAll('.mode-row label').forEach((l) => {\\n          l.classList.toggle('active', l.querySelector('input').checked);\\n        });\\n      });\\n    });\\n\\n    const thumbnails = [];\\n    for (let i = 1; i <= 9; i++) {\\n      const card = document.createElement('div');\\n      card.className = 'thumb-card';\\n      const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');\\n      const label = document.createElement('div');\\n      label.className = 'label';\\n      const text = document.createElement('span');\\n      text.textContent = 'Thumbnail ' + i;\\n      const resetBtn = document.createElement('button');\\n      resetBtn.className = 'reset-btn';\\n      resetBtn.textContent = 'reset';\\n      label.appendChild(text);\\n      label.appendChild(resetBtn);\\n      card.appendChild(canvas);\\n      card.appendChild(label);\\n      grid.appendChild(card);\\n\\n      const testImage = generateTestImage(i * 7 + 13, 256);\\n\\n      try {\\n        const wt = new WatercolorThumbnail(canvas, testImage, {\\n          clickToDeluge: true,\\n          getMode: () => currentMode,\\n          size: 256,\\n        });\\n        thumbnails.push(wt);\\n        resetBtn.addEventListener('click', (e) => {\\n          e.stopPropagation();\\n          wt.reset();\\n        });\\n      } catch (err) {\\n        console.error('Thumbnail init failed:', err);\\n        text.textContent = 'init failed';\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    window.thumbnails = thumbnails;\\n    window.WatercolorThumbnail = WatercolorThumbnail;\\n    window.generateTestImage = generateTestImage;\\n    console.log('Watercolor thumbnails initialized.');\\n    console.log('Click any thumbnail to deluge.');\\n    console.log('Try from console: thumbnails[0].deluge(0.5, 0.5, \\\"bloom\\\")');\\n    console.log('Use real JPGs: new WatercolorThumbnail(canvas, \\\"/path/to/image.jpg\\\", opts)');\\n  </script>\\n</body>\\n</html>\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/watercolor-thumbnails.html\"}"

    "type": "text",
    "text": "## What changed\n\n**No WebGL anywhere.** Every canvas operation goes through the 2D context. K-M math is applied per-pixel via `ImageData` manipulation in pure JS, ported line-for-line from the GLSL shader. The deluge animation runs in a rAF loop that recomposites the frame by sampling from a source ImageData with per-pixel displacement.\n\n**Identical math, different runtime.** `rgbToK`, `kmReflect`, the smoothstep rise/fade easing for deluges, the disperse-vs-bloom modes, the bell-curve falloff, the mirror-clamp for displaced UVs \u2014 all the same. Just JavaScript instead of GLSL.\n\n**Same persistence semantics.** State accumulates across clicks. After a deluge animation finishes, the current frame is baked as the new \"source state\" and subsequent deluges operate on it. `reset()` restores the original K-M'd state captured at load.\n\n## What you should see\n\n- 9 thumbnails showing softly-watercolored geometric shapes on cream paper.\n- Click any to disperse or bloom (per the radio buttons).\n- Effects persist across clicks; reset button restores the initial K-M'd state.\n- No black canvases anywhere.\n\n## Performance notes for the real use case\n\nPer-pixel ImageData manipulation is slower than WebGL, but well within budget for thumbnail-sized canvases:\n- 256\u00d7256 = 65,536 pixels per frame\n- ~15 ops per pixel during a deluge (displacement + bilinear sample + blend)\n- ~1M ops per frame per thumbnail\n- On an M-class CPU: roughly 1\u20132ms per frame per actively-animating thumbnail\n- With all 9 animating simultaneously: ~10-15ms per frame, still 60fps territory\n\nFor larger thumbnails (512\u00d7512), this would 4\u00d7 the cost. If you need bigger or many more, that's when the WebGL version becomes worth debugging. For typical portfolio thumbnail sizes (200-300px), this is overkill-fast.\n\n## On the WebGL failure\n\nI shipped three broken versions. The honest answer is that I don't know why WebGL was producing black canvases in your environment \u2014 my hypotheses (attribute location, K=0 case, alpha sandbox quirks) might all be partially true but the actual cause needed console output I never asked for clearly enough. If you want to revisit the WebGL version later for performance reasons, run this:\n\n```js\n// In console, after loading any of the previous WebGL versions:\nconst c = document.querySelector('.thumb-card canvas');\nconst gl = c.getContext('webgl2');\nconsole.log({\n  hasContext: !!gl,\n  attrs: gl && gl.getContextAttributes(),\n  extFloat: gl && gl.getExtension('EXT_color_buffer_float'),\n  extHalfFloat: gl && gl.getExtension('EXT_color_buffer_half_float'),\n});\n```\n\nThat output would tell us exactly which assumption I made wrong. But for now, the 2D version solves the actual problem: working watercolor thumbnails with deluge that you can use.\n\n## What to try next\n\nIf this works, plausible next steps:\n- **Use real JPGs** from your portfolio by passing URLs instead of `generateTestImage()`\n- **Tune the K-M look** \u2014 `applyKMToImageData` has the math; you can adjust `thickness` range or paper noise modulation for different aesthetics\n- **Add more deluge variants** \u2014 directional sweeps, multi-point bursts, spiral patterns\n- **Lazy-init** \u2014 only run K-M on thumbnails as they scroll into view (IntersectionObserver), so a long page doesn't K-M-process 50 images upfront\n\nLet me know which (if any) you want to pursue.",
